mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following month-over-month INDPRO growth are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears and downward earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint elevation in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% and acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) identifies a restrictive monetary stance that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is accelerating, positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a positive coefficient (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with elevated interest rates, month-over-month declines in real retail sales (RSAFS) signal that consumer demand is cracking under the weight of prior tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated interest rates
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, RSAFS declines > 1.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real corporate margins and forces reactive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow CPIAUCSL acceleration in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in new privately owned housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening residential investment and durable goods demand, which drives broad earnings expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average increases for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: HOUST growth episodes are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) confirms a synchronized expansion in output and labor income, amplifying equity risk premium compression and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: coordinated expansion in output and labor markets
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not produce statistically significant positive 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) tightens financial conditions and raises short-term discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
